text: Thank you, Matt, and good afternoon to all those listening in today.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: We delivered an outstanding start to the year driven by accelerating demand for AI infrastructure
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: across our portfolio. Growth was broad-based with every segment increasing year over year,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: led by 57% data center revenue growth. First quarter revenue increased 38% year over year to
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: 10.3 billion. Earnings grew more than 40% and free cash flow more than tripled to a record 2.6 billion
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: driven by significantly higher sales of epic CPUs, instinct GPUs, and rising processors.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: These results mark a clear inflection in our growth trajectory and a structural shift in our business.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Data center is now the primary driver of our revenue and earnings growth. And as AI adoption
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: scales, demand is increasing not only for accelerators, but also for the high performance CPUs that
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: power and orchestrate those workloads. Turning to our segments, data center revenue increased
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: 57% year over year to a record 5.8 billion, led by strong demand for our epic CPUs and instinct GPUs.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: In server, we delivered our fourth consecutive quarter of record server CPU revenue. Revenue
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: increased more than 50% year over year with sales to both cloud and enterprise customers,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: each growing more than 50%. Share gains accelerated year over year, reflecting the ramp of fifth gen
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: epic turn CPUs and continued strength of fourth gen epic processors across a wide range of workloads.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: In cloud, AI was the primary driver of growth in the quarter, as every major cloud provider
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: expanded their epic footprint to support a broad range of AI workloads, from general purpose
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: compute and data processing to head nodes for accelerators and emerging agentic applications.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Epic powered cloud instances increased nearly 50% year over year to more than 1,600,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: with instances optimized for virtually every enterprise workloads and expanded availability
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: across the largest global cloud providers. In enterprise, demand accelerated with record
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: revenue and record sell through in the quarter. We expanded our customer base with new wins across
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: financial services, healthcare, industrial and digital infrastructure companies, while also
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: building momentum with mid market and SMB customers. We are well positioned to continue
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: gaining share as more enterprises standardize on epic across on prem and hybrid environments
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: based on our leadership performance and TCO. Looking ahead, our sixth gen epic
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: business processor, built on our zen six architecture and two nanometer process technology,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: is designed to extend our leadership across cloud enterprise and AI workloads.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: The Venice family spans a broad set of CPUs optimized for throughput performance per watt
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: and performance per dollar, including varano, our first epic CPU purpose built for AI infrastructure.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Across the portfolio, Venice widens our competitive advantage, delivering substantially higher
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: performance per socket and per watt versus competitive x86 offerings, and more than two
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: x throughput per socket versus leading ARM based AI solutions. Customer demand is very strong
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: with more customers validating and rampant platforms at this stage than with any prior epic generation,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: and we remain on track to launch Venice later this year. Looking more broadly, we are seeing a
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: meaningful acceleration in customer demand driven by the rapid scaling of AI workloads across both
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: cloud and enterprise. In fencing and a gen thick AI are increasing the need for server CPU compute
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: as these workloads require additional CPU processing for orchestration data movement and parallel
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: execution in addition to serving as the headnotes for GPUs and accelerators.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: As a result, we are seeing both stronger near term demand and deeper engagement with customers
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: on long term capacity planning. At our financial analyst day in November, we outlined a server
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: CPU market growing at approximately 18% annually over the next three to five years.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Based on the demand signals we are seeing today and the structural increase in CPU compute
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: requirements driven by a gen thick AI, we now expect the server CPU Tim to grow at greater than 35%
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: annually reaching over $120 billion by 2030. In response to this demand, we are working
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: closely with our supply chain partners to meaningfully increase our wafer and back end capacities to
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: support this growth. As a result, we now expect server CPU revenue to grow by more than 70%
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: year over year in the second quarter with robust growth continuing through the second half of
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: 2026 and into 2027 as we ramp our next generation epic processors.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Now turning to our data center AI business, revenue grew by a significant double digit
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: percentage year over year as adoption of instinct accelerates across cloud enterprise,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: sovereign and super computing customers. We're seeing strong momentum as customers move from
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: pilots to large scale production deployments, particularly in inference where our leadership
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: memory capacity and bandwidth are key advantages. This momentum is driving deeper long term customer
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: engagements including large scale multi generation deployments. A key example is our expanded
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: strategic partnership with meta to deploy up to six gigawatts of AMD instinct GPUs spanning
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: several product generations. Our agreement includes a custom GPU accelerator based on our
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: MI 450 architecture co-design to support meta's next generation AI workloads.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Gertmints are on track to begin in the second half of the year leveraging our Helios RAC scale
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: architecture which integrates instinct GPUs with epic Venice CPUs to deliver fully optimized high
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: performance AI infrastructure. Together with our previously announced open AI partnership,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: these engagements position AMD as a core partner to the world's largest AI infrastructure builders
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: with deep co engineering relationships and multi year visibility into large scale deployments.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: More broadly, instinct adoption continues to expand across AI native and enterprise customers
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: for both training and inference workloads. Existing partners are expanding instincts across
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: broader set of workloads while a growing number of new partners are deploying production AI
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: workloads on instinct, highlighting the maturity of our hardware and software stack.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: On the software front, we continue to make strong progress with RACAM, improving performance,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: scalability and enabling customers to reach production faster. In our latest ML per results,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: MI 355X delivered strong competitive performance across the full suite with leadership results
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: in multiple categories. We also expanded day zero support for the leading open models including
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: the latest Google Gemma4 family, Quinn, Kimmy and others, enabling customers to deploy new models
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: quickly with optimized performance. To build on this momentum, we have significantly accelerated
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: our RACAM development cadence through increased software investments and agent based coding
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: workflows, enabling faster performance improvements and more rapid deployment of new capabilities.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Looking ahead, customer pull for Helios is very strong driven by our leadership performance,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: memory bandwidth and scale out capacity. Helios development is progressing well with strong
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: execution across silicon software and systems as we advance through key milestones.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: We have begun sampling MI 450 series GPUs to lead customers and remain on track to ramp
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Helios production shipments in the second half of the year. As we approach production, demand
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: for MI 450 series GPUs continues to strengthen with lead customer forecasts now exceeding our initial
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: plans and a growing number of new customers engaging on large scale deployments including
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: additional multi gigawatt opportunities. With this expanded visibility, we have strong and
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: increasing confidence in our ability to deliver tens of billions of dollars in annual data center
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: AI revenue in 2027 and to exceed our long-term growth target of greater than 80 percent in the
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: coming years. I look forward to sharing more on our next generation Instinct GPUs, Epic processors,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Helios Rack scale platform and our growing customer engagements at our advancing AI event in July.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Turning to client and gaming, segment revenue increased 23 percent year over year to 3.6 billion.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Ryzen processors and continued share gains across consumer and commercial markets.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: In desktop, we strengthened our Ryzen lineup including our latest X3D processors that deliver
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: leadership performance across gaming content creation and professional workloads. We also
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: introduced the Ryzen AI 400 series and Ryzen AI Pro 400 series desktop CPUs, sending our AI PC
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: offerings across both consumer and commercial systems. In mobile, we delivered strong growth
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: driven by a richer product mix as Ryzen 400 mobile PC shipments ramped and commercial adoption
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: increased. Commercial was a key highlight in the quarter with sell through of Ryzen Pro PCs
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: increasing more than 50 percent year over year as Dell, HP and Lenovo broadened their AMD offerings.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: We also closed new enterprise wins across large technology, financial services, healthcare and
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: second quarter. However, we are planning for second half PC shipments to be lower due to higher memory
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: and component costs. Against this backdrop, we still expect our client revenue to grow year over
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: year and outperform the market driven by the strength of our Ryzen portfolio and expanding
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: commercial adoption. In gaming, revenue increased 11 percent year over year to 720 million.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Semi-custom revenue declined year over year as expected at this stage of the console cycle,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: while engagements with customers on next generation platforms remain strong.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: In graphics, revenue increased year over year led by demand for our latest generation Radeon
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: 9000 series GPUs. We also strengthened our Radeon portfolio with updates to our FSR software that
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: memory and component costs. And we are planning the business accordingly.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Turning to our embedded segment, revenue increased 6 percent year over year to 873 million,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: driven by strength in test, measurement and emulation, aerospace and defense and communications,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: wins across markets, reflecting the continued expansion of our embedded business from a primarily
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: FPGA-focused portfolio to a broader set of adaptive embedded x86 and semi-custom solutions
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: significantly expanding our TAM. Our semi-custom engagement also expanded in the quarter as data
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: center communications and other embedded customers leverage our broad IP portfolio and high performance
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: expertise to build differentiated solutions. In summary, our first quarter results mark a
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: clear step up in our growth trajectory with accelerating momentum across the business.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: Our client business continues to outperform the market driven by rise in adoption and share
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: gains while an embedded design win momentum and demand are strengthening across our expanded
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: adaptive and x86 portfolio. At the same time, our data center business is inflecting with strong
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: demand for both epic and instant products driving significant growth. While we are still on the
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: early stages of the AI infrastructure cycle, the pace and scale of deployments we are seeing today
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: reinforce both the magnitude and durability of the opportunity ahead. As influencing an
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: agentic AI deployment scale, they are fundamentally increasing compute requirements,
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: driving both larger scale accelerator deployments and significantly more CPU compute.
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: AMD is uniquely positioned to lead in this next phase of AI with leadership products across
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

text: high performance service CPUs and AI accelerators and the ability to optimize them together as
speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO

speaker: DR. LISA SU - CHAIR AND CEO
